title: Fix duplicate wordList useEffect in ExploreCards + create verify-assets script

files_to_modify.reason: Merge two useEffects (lines ~30-51 and ~53-59) into a single effect that loads words from prop or DB, sets state once, and calls audioManager.preloadWords. Add cleanup flag to prevent setState on unmounted component.
Add 'verify-assets' script ('tsx scripts/verify-assets.ts') and 'prebuild' script so asset verification runs before every production build.

files_to_create.reason: New prebuild guard script that checks existence of every vocabulary word's image PNG and two audio MP3 files, prints a per-word PASS/FAIL table, and exits 1 if anything is missing.

approach: A — Conservative: minimal, targeted changes. Keep existing component structure; only merge the two useEffects into one and wire in audioManager.preloadWords. New script follows exact patterns already established in scripts/generate-*.ts.
steps: 2. Read ExploreCards.tsx; identify the two useEffects and their shared dependency surface (db, wordList).

3. Replace the two useEffects with a single merged useEffect using a cancelled flag. Logic: if wordList prop is provided use it directly; otherwise call getAllWords(db) (plus getSetting for childName in the same Promise.all). After resolving, call audioManager.preloadWords(words).
4. Verify the dependency array is [db, wordList] and the childName fetch is still covered inside the merged effect.

5. Create scripts/verify-assets.ts following the exact import/structure described in the task. Use existsSync from 'fs', join from 'path', VOCABULARY_SEED from '../src/lib/vocabulary-seed'. Project root = process.cwd(). Check image at {root}{word.imagePath}, audioEn at {root}/public{word.audioEnPath}, audioZh at {root}/public{word.audioZhPath}. Print table and exit codes.
6. Add 'verify-assets': 'tsx scripts/verify-assets.ts' and 'prebuild': 'tsx scripts/verify-assets.ts' to package.json scripts.
7. Run 'npx tsx scripts/verify-assets.ts' to see actual results.
8. If images missing, run 'npx tsx scripts/generate-images.ts'.
9. If English audio missing, run 'npx tsx scripts/generate-audio-en.ts'.
10. If Mandarin audio missing, run 'npx tsx scripts/generate-audio-zh.ts'.
12. Run 'npm test' — verify ExploreCards.test.tsx still passes (no new failures).
13. Commit all source changes + any newly generated assets.
expected_tests: ExploreCards.test.tsx (7 existing tests) — all must continue to pass after the useEffect merge

No new unit tests required: the change is a refactor of two effects into one with identical external behavior. The existing test suite already covers loading from prop (wordList fixture), rendering first card, navigation, audio playback, and completion overlay.
risk_areas: childName loading: currently fetched in BOTH effects. The merged effect must still fetch it (it is inside the DB branch of the merged effect as part of the existing Promise.all). Risk: LOW — code already has this pattern.

audioManager.preloadWords in a server-side / test environment: audioManager uses Howler which is mocked in __mocks__. Need to verify the mock exposes preloadWords or add a no-op. Risk: LOW — mock already exists; may need a one-line addition.
Cleanup flag: must be a let variable inside useEffect, not a ref, to work correctly in React StrictMode double-invocation. Risk: LOW — pattern is straightforward.
verify-assets.ts path resolution: imagePath vs audio path differ (audio uses /public prefix). Risk: LOW — task description is explicit; follow it exactly.
prebuild script: if any asset is missing it exits 1 and blocks 'npm run build'. This is intentional but could surprise CI if assets haven't been generated. Risk: LOW — this is the stated requirement.
persistence_strategy: N/A for this task — data is already persisted in IndexedDB (vocabulary) and public/ directory (static assets). No new persistence mechanism is introduced.
error_handling_approach: ExploreCards already has try/catch around the DB fetch with console.warn and isLoading fallback. The merged effect preserves this. verify-assets.ts is a Node.js script (not a React component) — it uses existsSync (synchronous, no async errors) and exits 1 on missing assets. No try/catch needed beyond what exists.
assumptions: The second useEffect (lines 53-59) that re-fetches childName is redundant because the first effect already fetches it inside the same Promise.all. Merging removes this duplicate call.
audioManager.preloadWords is called with the final resolved words array (after shuffling and slicing), not with the raw DB result.
The __mocks__ for audioManager either already include preloadWords or need a trivial no-op addition.

The imagePath in VOCABULARY_SEED already starts with '/' and does NOT include '/public', so the image check uses {root}{imagePath} (which resolves outside public/). The audio paths also start with '/' and the check prefixes '/public'.
process.cwd() when running 'npx tsx scripts/verify-assets.ts' from the project root equals the project root. Using process.cwd() is more reliable than __dirname for tsx scripts.
